Bunch Sprint In Final Outing Before Flanders! | Dwars Door Vlaanderen 2022 Women's Highlights

transcript.clean_text: hot on the heels of the panna the e3 and again well again it's dwayz dor vlanderen the 76th edition of this one day semi classic in the florida's region of belgium and the final build up towards the ronda van vlander and this coming sunday 184 kilometers the total distance between the start in rusella and the finish in waragan with 13 climbs and six cobbled rows to be dealt with along the way as they crisscross the flemish ardennes it took 30 kilometers before an early break managed to go clear it consisted of five riders niels pollitt of borah hansgrah kello o'brien of bike exchange johann jacobs of movie star aaron the villas of sport blender and balawas and my taste bashkins of bingo power sources behind them though alperson fenix and matthew vanderbilt had already lit things up with 90 k's remaining their acceleration though served as a launch pad for three riders from rival teams archaea samsung's connor swift lewis ascii from group armour and brent van muer of lotto sue dal well as they race towards the toughest climb of the day the nerves inevitably led to a crash alperson fenix's michael gogl on the ground amongst many others the big moment of the race though came on the bergton outer climb freshly cobbled back in 2018 and with some really tough gradients it saw ineos grenadiers on the offensive with ben turner and tom pidcock catching and passing the group of van moore they drew a group clear that also included vanderpool tispanot of jumbo visma stefan kung of group armor and victor camponat of lotto sudal over the top of that climb the gaps were small but with groups all over the road it was going to be hard for anybody to close them on to the next climb of the canardiburg and it was golden greg van avermaet attempting to do just that but over the top of him went today pagatcha who'd been caught out of position just before his rivals sailed up the road earlier the two-time tour champion was racing for the first time over the cobbles as a pro rider but his power looked to be outweighing his inexperience as he closed the gap to 10 seconds but that was as good as it got he couldn't get any closer and a few k's further down the road he was caught by the main peloton and soon after that crash there was a big crash a touch of wheels saw oliver nason hit the deck hard that one looked particularly painful on to the knocktoberg and it was jan trapnic on the move for bahrain victorious closely followed by valentin madua of group armor over the top of that particular climb crow anderson pagacha van avenmart and cockar had joined them and there was just a matter of a 20-second gap to close to the group in front that had risen to 24 as the early breakaway was caught and the increase in pace spelled the end of the time out front for the vilst with bernout continuing to accelerate out front the next riders get himself into trouble was jakobs also having spent the whole day in the breakaway into the closing 30ks and pagatcha still hadn't given up accelerating over the dawn section of cobbles but by this point the gap to the front had expanded to over 40 seconds you've got to admire him though he's won many of the biggest races in the world but he never ever gives up the closing 10ks were nail biting if you can watch them on demand over on g sim plus i thoroughly recommend you do so stefan kuhn gave it a nudge on the 13th and final climb of the day but it was victor camponats on the descent that got the first gap reportedly using a 62 front chain ring he was gritting his teeth so hard in fact that he chipped one of them in the process but oates and pigcott would later bridge across then vanderpool and we were back to a group of eight kavanaugh simply would not give up though going again inside four k's to go with only bernout going after him they too were caught though but once again we would see the belgian rider from jumbo visma on the attack again pidcot was also in attendance but at this point burnout did get a gap vanderpool was forced to chase behind him and the other riders in the group all looked at each other so two riders out front with one and a half k's remaining and it would be those two who came into the closing finishing straight together to fight it out for the win vanderbilt pushing it almost too hard in the final right hand hurt turn he went with 250 meters still remaining benoit did the best that he possibly could but he was no match for the dutchman in the final sprint of the line win number two then for mata vanderpool in just seven days of road racing this season he's back and he's going to be a force to be reckoned with this coming sunday tom pidcock took third place on the podium just ahead of victor kampernitz who took fourth well he took the win but it clearly took a lot out of him matthew vanderpool taking his first ever world tour win here three years ago in 2019 and he's added another win to his list today well here's the top ten of the day vanderpool taking it ahead of bernout pidcock campernauts polit stefan cooney sixth kellen o'brien with a brilliant ride in seventh turner trafnic and pagatcha rounding out the top ten and this is quite the final podium isn't it all three of these rides will likely play a key role at the tour de flanders later on this week make sure that you join us on g sim plus for live coverage from kilometer zero and indeed for the breakaway show before and after the race i will see you bright and early on sunday you
